title: aita for sabotaging the rehoming of me 10 year old dog despite my parents' wishes to get rid of him?
text: for context, i (17f) have had this dog since i was seven years old.  i have always taken care of him the most out of anyone and he's always gravitated towards me, and seldom paid much mind to the rest of the family.  i struggle with multiple mental illnesses including bpd, ptsd, anxiety, depression, and ocd.  my dog has been the best companion i've ever had during the lows of those illnesses and i can honestly say he plays a huge part in why i'm here today. last week my world was turned upside down when my parents decided to tell me that they were going to rehome my dog.   instantly, i was crying.  i couldn't hold back the tears if my life depended on it.  then i heard the reason why they were giving away my dog and i was furious. for a long time, my eight year old sister has wanted a dog of her own, but my parents don't want the burden of having two dogs, so obviously the best option for them was to exchange the 10 year companion of their mentally ill daughter for a new one like he was some kind of accessory or replaceable object instead of just saying no to my sister for once. i tried having a mature conversation with them despite how furious i was.  i said "i know she's wanted a dog for a long time, but trading a companion that's been with me for most of my life and has been the reason why i'm here today should be out of the question."  they wouldn't listen to me, they accused me of being jealous and resenting my sister, and overall i got nowhere with that conversation.  i felt incredibly dismissed. a few days later i found an ad of my dog on kijiji.  it had been up for two days at that point, so i thought it'd be long enough for it to be believable that someone has already bought the dog.  i went on the family computer and notified all who would see the ad that my dog had already been adopted so that there wouldn't be any more offers and anyone who offered beforehand thought that my parents had simply chosen another family to give my dog to. my parents found out about that yesterday, and came into my room screaming at me, calling me selfish and entitled.  i was pretty apathetic to anything that they had to say so all i responded with was "i told you he's my dog, and if you two don't do anything to take care of him you should have no say in whether he stays or goes." my family hasn't spoken to me after that, and honestly i couldn't care less.  my dog is not an accessory, he's a living being who means the world to me and i won't let anyone treat him as any less than that regardless of who they are.  aita in this situation?

storyboard.content: I'm 17 years old and I've had my dog since I was seven. I have always taken care of him the most out of anyone in the family, and he's always gravitated toward me.
I struggle with multiple mental illnesses including BPD, PTSD, anxiety, depression, and OCD. My dog has been my best companion during the lows and plays a huge part in why I'm here today.
My parents decided to rehome my 10-year-old dog because my 8-year-old sister wants a dog of her own and they don't want the burden of having two dogs.
I tried having a mature conversation with my parents, telling them that trading a companion who's been with me for most of my life should be out of the question.
My parents wouldn't listen to me. They accused me of being jealous and resenting my sister, and I got nowhere with the conversation. I felt incredibly dismissed.
I found the ad for my dog on Kijiji and went on the family computer to notify everyone that the dog had already been adopted, so there wouldn't be any more offers.
The ad had been up for two days, so I thought it would be believable that someone had already bought the dog.
My parents found out and came into my room screaming at me, calling me selfish and entitled. I told them he's my dog and they have no say since they don't take care of him.
My family hasn't spoken to me since, but honestly I couldn't care less.
